content.problemStatement:
Chef bought NNN items from a shop. Although it is hard to carry all these items in hand, so Chef has to buy some polybags to store these items.

111 polybag can contain at most 101010 items. What is the minimum number of polybags needed by Chef?


Input Format

The first line will contain an integer TTT - number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first and only line of each test case contains an integer NNN - the number of items bought by Chef.


Output Format
For each test case, output the minimum number of polybags required.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N, the number of items Chef bought.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the minimum number of polybags required.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For N=20, ceil(20/10)=2 bags. For N=24, ceil(24/10)=3 bags. For N=99, ceil(99/10)=10 bags.
Exactly 10 items fit into one polybag.

solution.approach: Each polybag can hold at most 10 items. The smallest number of bags needed for N items is ceil(N/10). This can be computed without floating point by (N + 9) / 10 using integer division. Process each test case independently.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables.

code.Python3:
import sys
data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
if not data:
    sys.exit()
T = int(data[0])
out = []
idx = 1
for _ in range(T):
    N = int(data[idx]); idx += 1
    bags = (N + 9) // 10
    out.append(str(bags))
print('\n'.join(out))
